title: Collomia aggregata
taxonomy: Collomia aggregata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Collomia, which includes several species of annual and perennial plants. The family Polemoniaceae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the phlox family. Collomia aggregata is native to the western United States and is found in a variety of habitats, including meadows and woodlands. The plant is often found growing in clusters or aggregations, hence its common name.
uses: Collomia aggregata, also known as Cliff Mass, is a flowering plant native to western North America. intro: Collomia aggregata, also known as Cliff Mountain Mallow or Purple Collomia, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Collomia aggregata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
